name: All America Bank
description_short: Community bank serving Oklahoma since 1927, offering personal and business banking, lending, and deposit products with competitive rates and local relationship-based service.
description_long:
All America Bank is a community bank headquartered in Oklahoma with roots tracing back to 1927, when T.C. Huckabay began his banking career at Planter's State Bank in Mountain Park. The bank has evolved through multiple acquisitions and mergers, most notably the 2015 consolidation of All America Bank, Bank of the Wichitas, and Southwest State Bank under the All America Bank name. Today, the bank operates as a family-owned institution now in its fourth generation of Huckabay family leadership, with Todd Huckabay serving as Chief Executive Officer and Wade Huckabay as President.

All America Bank offers a comprehensive suite of banking and lending products. On the deposit side, they provide checking accounts (including the Mega Money Market account ranked #1 by depositaccounts.com with 3.85% APY and Ultimate Rewards Checking at 4.10% APY), savings accounts, CDs, and IRAs. For lending, they offer home loans, consumer loans, business loans, and agricultural financing. Additional services include credit cards, online and mobile banking, wallet pay functionality, safe deposit boxes, direct deposit, money wiring, and bank-to-bank transfers for business customers.

The bank explicitly differentiates itself through several operational practices: customers always speak directly with real people rather than automated systems, loan processing and decisions are kept in-house (mortgages and loans are never sold to secondary markets), and they emphasize fast turnaround times on loan approvals. They position themselves on offering the highest deposit rates possible and claim to reward customer loyalty and business. Their service philosophy emphasizes simplicity and avoiding red tape, with a stated commitment to personal service.

All America Bank is a legitimate, established regional financial institution with nearly 100 years of operating history and transparent family ownership. However, as a community bank, their service area and product offerings are primarily regional to Oklahoma rather than nationwide. The website does not provide comprehensive details on fees, minimum balance requirements, credit requirements for loans, or full terms of their deposit products, which are typical details customers would need for comparison shopping.
services: Personal checking accounts (Mega Money Market and Ultimate Rewards Checking)
Online banking and mobile banking app
pros: Mega Money Market account ranked #1 'Best Money Market account in the USA' by depositaccounts.com with 3.85% APY
Ultimate Rewards Checking account offers 4.10% APY with stated benefits
Decades-long banking history (since 1927) with transparent fourth-generation family ownership and leadership
All personal, business, and agricultural loans kept in-house—never sold to secondary market, enabling faster decisions
Commitment to speaking with real people rather than automated phone systems
Offers both personal and business banking products including ag financing specific to Oklahoma region
Online and mobile banking with mobile deposit and bill pay functionality
cons: Regional Oklahoma-focused bank with limited geographic footprint compared to national banks
Website does not clearly disclose account fees, minimum balance requirements, or full terms for deposit products
Limited details on credit requirements, APRs, or terms for consumer and business loan products
No information provided about FDIC insurance coverage or deposit insurance limits
Agricultural financing niche product suggests limited product depth in some lending categories versus larger institutions

contact.address: 200 S Rockwell Ave, Oklahoma City, OK 73128

best_for: Oklahoma residents seeking competitive deposit rates with local relationship banking
Small business owners and agricultural enterprises in Oklahoma needing relationship-based lending
Customers prioritizing in-house loan decisions and direct contact with loan officers over automated processing
Depositors seeking money market or high-yield checking accounts in the regional market
diagnosis: All America Bank is best for Oklahoma-based customers and small business owners who value personalized service, competitive deposit rates, and in-house loan decisions over the convenience of national branch networks. The main caveat is that this is a regional bank with limited geographic reach, and critical product details (fees, minimums, loan terms, credit requirements) are not fully disclosed on their website, requiring direct contact with the bank for complete comparison.
typical_results_timeline: Account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions vary by product.
address: 200 S Rockwell Ave, Oklahoma City, OK 73128
